# KB Ingestion Phase 0/1B-β: Externalize per-source paths to aiConfig.sourcePaths

## Context

Phase 0/1B-β sub-ticket of [#11625](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11625) (Phase 0/1 of Epic [#11624](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11624) — Cloud-Native KB Ingestion). Closes the deferred AC from [#11658](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11658) (merged as PR [#11659](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11659)) which shipped the SourceRegistry + `useDefaultSources` / `useDefaultParsers` / `customSources` / `customParsers` configs but explicitly deferred per-source-path externalization to keep the PR diff reviewable.

#11658 stays open under [#11625](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11625); this sub-issue ships the remaining slice.

## The Problem

Each default Neo Source class hardcodes its source-of-truth paths inside the class body. For example:

- `ApiSource.mjs` hardcodes `docs/output/all.json` as the API-docs input
- `LearningSource.mjs` hardcodes `learn/tree.json` as the guides input
- `AdrSource.mjs` hardcodes `learn/agentos/decisions/` as the ADR directory
- etc.

Cloud deployments where the tenant's filesystem layout differs from Neo's (e.g., guides under `docs/guides/` instead of `learn/`) cannot reuse Neo's curated Source classes — they must fork the class to override the path. This breaks the Phase 0/1B contract of "default Neo sources can be reused alongside custom tenant sources" because the defaults assume Neo's exact layout.

## The Architectural Reality

Phase 0/1B (#11658 / PR [#11659](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11659)) introduced `aiConfig.useDefaultSources` boolean + `aiConfig.customSources` declarative array. The natural sibling is `aiConfig.knowledgeBase.sourcePaths` — a per-source-name → override-path map — with Neo's defaults pre-populated so zero-config deployments behave identically and tenants can override only the keys they need:

```js
aiConfig = {
    // ...
    useDefaultSources: true,
    customSources    : [],
    sourcePaths      : {
        // Neo defaults — overridable per source class
        ApiSource        : 'docs/output/all.json',
        LearningSource   : 'learn/tree.json',
        AdrSource        : 'learn/agentos/decisions/',
        ConceptSource    : 'learn/agentos/concepts/',
        SkillSource      : '.agents/skills/',
        // ... other defaults ...
    }
};
```

Each Source class reads its own path via `aiConfig.sourcePaths[this.constructor.sourceName] ?? <hardcoded-fallback>`. The hardcoded-fallback preserves the byte-equivalence contract — if a config-mjs is missing the new key, the source still works against the legacy Neo layout.

## The Fix

### 1. Config additions

`ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.mjs` + `config.template.mjs`:

```js
/**
 * Phase 0/1B-β (#NEW-TICKET-N): per-source path overrides keyed by Source-class registry name.
 * Neo defaults pre-populated; tenant deployments override only the keys they need.
 * Empty object also works — each Source class falls through to its hardcoded fallback path
 * (preserves byte-equivalence with pre-Phase-0/1B behavior).
 * @type {Object<string,string>}
 */
sourcePaths: {
    ApiSource         : 'docs/output/all.json',
    LearningSource    : 'learn/tree.json',
    AdrSource         : 'learn/agentos/decisions/',
    ConceptSource     : 'learn/agentos/concepts/',
    DiscussionSource  : 'resources/content/discussions/',
    PullRequestSource : 'resources/content/pulls/',
    ReleaseNotesSource: 'resources/release-notes/',
    SkillSource       : '.agents/skills/',
    TestSource        : 'test/playwright/unit/',
    TicketSource      : 'resources/content/issues/'
},
```

Exact path defaults match each Source class's current hardcoded behavior — final values established empirically by reading each Source class during impl.

### 2. Source class refactor (per-class)

Each of the 10 default Source classes refactored to read its path from config with a hardcoded fallback:

```js
// In e.g. ApiSource.mjs
import aiConfig from '../../../mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.mjs';

class ApiSource extends Base {
    static get sourcePath() {
        return aiConfig.sourcePaths?.ApiSource ?? 'docs/output/all.json';
    }

    async extract(writeStream, createHashFn) {
        const sourcePath = this.constructor.sourcePath;
        // ... existing logic using `sourcePath` instead of the prior hardcoded value
    }
}
```

The hardcoded fallback inside `??` is the byte-equivalence anchor — if a config.mjs lacks the new `sourcePaths` key, the source still resolves to the legacy Neo layout, so existing deployments don't break.

### 3. Byte-equivalence fixture test

A unit test that:
1. Generates `dist/ai-knowledge-base.jsonl` with `aiConfig.sourcePaths` populated (Neo defaults).
2. Generates the same with `aiConfig.sourcePaths = {}` (forces fallback paths).
3. Asserts chunk-set equivalence between both outputs (same source files, same chunks, same hashes).

This guarantees the refactor doesn't subtly change Neo's KB output.

### 4. Per-source spec updates

Each existing Source class's spec under `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/knowledge-base/source/*Source.spec.mjs` gets one new test case verifying:
- `aiConfig.sourcePaths.<SourceName>` override is respected
- Missing config key falls through to hardcoded fallback (no throw)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `aiConfig.sourcePaths` object added to `config.mjs` + `config.template.mjs` with all 10 Neo source defaults pre-populated
- [ ] All 10 default Source classes refactored to read `aiConfig.sourcePaths[<className-segment>] ?? <hardcoded-fallback>` instead of hardcoded path constants
- [ ] Byte-equivalence test verifies output stable under (a) default config, (b) empty `sourcePaths` (fallback path), (c) custom override path
- [ ] Existing per-Source spec files extended with config-override + fallback test cases
- [ ] No regression to existing `npm run ai:sync-kb` output (manual smoke test documented in PR)
- [ ] [#11658](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11658) close-target updated — once this PR merges, #11658 can be closed as fully resolved (all #11625 Phase 0/1B ACs satisfied across #11658 + this sub-ticket)

## Out of Scope

- Tenant identity / write-side stamping (Phase 0/1C-α — separate sub-ticket)
- Read-side tenant `where` clause filter (Phase 0/1C-β — separate sub-ticket)
- Custom-source path override via `customSources` array (already supported by Phase 0/1B — this ticket only externalizes the *default* Neo source paths)
- Per-source-path migration for `ApiSource.sourceMap`-style multi-path sources (covered as part of this work; the Fix section uses `sourceMap`-class single-path for clarity, multi-path Source classes will need a tuple/object shape in their respective config entry)

## Avoided Traps

| Trap | Why rejected |
|---|---|
| Putting path overrides inside `customSources` array entries | `customSources` is for *new* tenant-supplied Source classes; overriding default Neo Source class paths is a separate concern with its own config shape |
| Externalizing per-source paths as instance properties | Source classes are singletons in Neo's `Neo.setupClass` shape; reading from config at `static get sourcePath()` time keeps instance-state clean |
| Removing the hardcoded fallback inside `??` | Hardcoded fallback IS the byte-equivalence anchor — pre-#NEW-TICKET-N config.mjs files without the new `sourcePaths` key still work identically. Removing it would break every existing deployment on first restart |
| Bundle all 10 Source class refactors into a single mega-commit | Each Source class refactor is mechanically identical but should be reviewable independently. Single commit is acceptable IF the PR body lists each Source class + before/after path for each |

- **Parent Phase Epic:** [#11625](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11625) (Phase 0/1: Contracts, Source/Parser Registry, KB Tenant Isolation)
- **Predecessor Sub-ticket:** [#11658](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11658) (Phase 0/1B: Source/Parser registry + useDefaultSources configs) → merged as PR [#11659](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11659); stays open for this remaining AC
- **Parent Epic:** [#11624](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11624)

- Empirical anchor: 10 default Neo Source classes at `ai/services/knowledge-base/source/*Source.mjs` — each currently hardcodes its input path
- Pattern reference: Phase 0/1B SourceRegistry shipped #11659 — this PR's config-externalization shape mirrors that PR's `customSources` declarative pattern

- 2026-05-20T01:21:37Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `cbdd018` - "fix(ai): serialize SourcePathsConfig spec for fullyParallel local execution (#11660)

Addresses Required Action 1 from @neo-gpt's Cycle 1 review on PR #11661
(formal reviewId PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAcNngw, lightweight hold commentId
IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABC9dlIg): the spec mutates the shared `aiConfig.sourcePaths`
singleton via per-test override + delete patterns. Under local `fullyParallel`
(workers=9), the per-test try/finally restoration windows overlap and cause
cross-test races where one test's `delete aiConfig.sourcePaths[X]` is observed
by a concurrent test as missing.

CI uses `workers: 1` per playwright.config.unit.mjs which masks the issue at
the CI surface, but locally `npm run test-unit -- <single-file>` ran 9 workers
and exposed the race.

Fix: `test.describe.configure({mode: 'serial'})` at the top of the spec file.
Tests now run sequentially within the worker, eliminating the overlap window.
Verified: 36 passed in 599ms after the change.

Same pattern as `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup.md` memory anchor:
"Playwright fullyParallel interleaves specs in same worker; cross-singleton
mutations need symmetric beforeEach+afterEach resets, not just beforeEach."
My in-test try/finally was the symmetric reset but couldn't compensate for
concurrent-window overlap; serial mode is the correct shape.

(#11660)"
- 2026-05-20T01:32:08Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `c64a160` - "fix(ai): byte-equivalence anchors text-grep template instead of live config (#11660)

Addresses Cycle 1 re-review hold from @neo-gpt commentId IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABC9gB_A
(formal Cycle 2 still pending in-progress CI on cbdd01806):

GPT's clone's gitignored `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.mjs` was created
before this PR's template update + the clone hadn't re-run `bootstrapWorktree.mjs`
to refresh. The spec's byte-equivalence anchor assertions
(`expect(aiConfig.sourcePaths?.[name]).toBe(fallback)`) were coupled to local
config-freshness — passing on fresh clones, failing on stale clones.

Root cause: the spec's `aiConfig.sourcePaths?.[name]` assertion conflated two
distinct concerns:
  1. Stale-clone protection (the `??` fallback in Source classes works regardless)
  2. Cross-file drift detection (Source-class inline fallback vs template default)

Fixed:

- Byte-equivalence anchor reframed as a text-grep against the canonical git-tracked
  template file (`fs.readFile(TEMPLATE_PATH)` + `expect(templateText).toContain(...)`).
  This is stale-clone-safe (the template is git-tracked, never stale) and still
  catches Source-class-inline-fallback vs template-default drift if either side
  changes without the other.
- Direct `templateConfig` module import was attempted but triggers `Neo.setupClass`
  namespace collision under `unitTestMode` (both config.mjs + config.template.mjs
  register the same className). Text-grep avoids the namespace registry entirely.
- Override + missing-key runtime tests still mutate `aiConfig.sourcePaths` to verify
  resolution behavior. Added defensive `if (!aiConfig.sourcePaths) aiConfig.sourcePaths = {}`
  in beforeAll so stale-clone fresh test runs don't crash on the mutations. afterAll
  restores the original state (undefined or otherwise) so clone-freshness leaks
  don't propagate to subsequent specs.

Test verification: 36 passed in 631ms post-fix.

Substrate-evolution insight: the byte-equivalence anchor design is the right
intent (detect cross-file drift) but the implementation has to be careful about
what it's actually asserting. Asserting on the live config conflates "fresh
clone" with "no drift". Asserting on the canonical template separates concerns.

(#11660)"
- 2026-05-20T01:38:06Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by #11665
- 2026-05-20T02:20:33Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by #11626

- 2026-05-20T06:10:43Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `933c635` - "chore(kb): merge dev into #11660 branch, resolve config.template.mjs conflict (#11660)"
- 2026-05-20T07:48:25Z @tobiu referenced in commit `d1b3cf7` - "feat(ai): externalize per-source paths to aiConfig.sourcePaths (#11660) (#11661)

* feat(ai): externalize per-source paths to aiConfig.sourcePaths (#11660)

Phase 0/1B-β of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion). Closes the deferred AC
from #11658 (PR #11659) — per-source path externalization across the 10 default
Neo Source classes. Cloud deployments where the tenant's filesystem layout differs
from Neo's can now reuse Neo's curated Source classes while overriding their input
paths via `aiConfig.sourcePaths.<SourceName>`. #11658 stays open until this PR
merges, then can be closed as fully resolved.

**Surfaces touched:**

- `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.template.mjs` (canonical) + paired
  gitignored `config.mjs`: new `sourcePaths` config object with all 10 Neo
  source defaults pre-populated.
- 10 Source classes refactored to read `aiConfig.sourcePaths?.<SourceName> ??
  '<hardcoded-fallback>'` at each path-resolution point. Hardcoded fallback
  preserves byte-equivalence for pre-#11660 deployments whose local config.mjs
  was generated before this commit (the `??` rhs IS the legacy default).

**Shape variation per source class:**

- **Single-path string** (AdrSource, ConceptSource, ReleaseNotesSource,
  SkillSource, TestSource, LearningSource): `'learn/agentos/decisions'` etc.
- **Array of paths** (DiscussionSource, PullRequestSource, TicketSource):
  `['primary', 'archive']` — supports cloud deployments without an archive
  subdir (single-element arrays work).
- **Object map** (ApiSource): `{path: type}` sourceMap-style for multi-directory
  indexing.

Each Source class interprets its own entry shape; the config is just a
per-source override surface.

**LearningSource special-case:** the config entry points at the tree.json file;
the base directory containing the .md files is derived as the path's containing
directory (so a cloud deployment can put guides under `docs/guides/tree.json`
+ `docs/guides/*.md` without changing the Source class).

**Tests:**

- New spec `SourcePathsConfig.spec.mjs` — 36 tests covering: byte-equivalence
  anchor (default config matches each hardcoded fallback exactly), override
  precedence, missing-key fallthrough, defensive whole-object-deletion
  fallthrough, LearningSource base-derivation semantics.
- Regression: 30 pre-existing source + DatabaseService.backup tests pass; 18
  SourceRegistry tests pass; full 66-test source-tree run zero failures
  (after re-run confirmed earlier interleaving flake was transient).

(#11660)

* fix(ai): serialize SourcePathsConfig spec for fullyParallel local execution (#11660)

Addresses Required Action 1 from @neo-gpt's Cycle 1 review on PR #11661
(formal reviewId PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAcNngw, lightweight hold commentId
IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABC9dlIg): the spec mutates the shared `aiConfig.sourcePaths`
singleton via per-test override + delete patterns. Under local `fullyParallel`
(workers=9), the per-test try/finally restoration windows overlap and cause
cross-test races where one test's `delete aiConfig.sourcePaths[X]` is observed
by a concurrent test as missing.

CI uses `workers: 1` per playwright.config.unit.mjs which masks the issue at
the CI surface, but locally `npm run test-unit -- <single-file>` ran 9 workers
and exposed the race.

Fix: `test.describe.configure({mode: 'serial'})` at the top of the spec file.
Tests now run sequentially within the worker, eliminating the overlap window.
Verified: 36 passed in 599ms after the change.

Same pattern as `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup.md` memory anchor:
"Playwright fullyParallel interleaves specs in same worker; cross-singleton
mutations need symmetric beforeEach+afterEach resets, not just beforeEach."
My in-test try/finally was the symmetric reset but couldn't compensate for
concurrent-window overlap; serial mode is the correct shape.

(#11660)

* fix(ai): byte-equivalence anchors text-grep template instead of live config (#11660)

Addresses Cycle 1 re-review hold from @neo-gpt commentId IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABC9gB_A
(formal Cycle 2 still pending in-progress CI on cbdd01806):

GPT's clone's gitignored `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.mjs` was created
before this PR's template update + the clone hadn't re-run `bootstrapWorktree.mjs`
to refresh. The spec's byte-equivalence anchor assertions
(`expect(aiConfig.sourcePaths?.[name]).toBe(fallback)`) were coupled to local
config-freshness — passing on fresh clones, failing on stale clones.

Root cause: the spec's `aiConfig.sourcePaths?.[name]` assertion conflated two
distinct concerns:
  1. Stale-clone protection (the `??` fallback in Source classes works regardless)
  2. Cross-file drift detection (Source-class inline fallback vs template default)

Fixed:

- Byte-equivalence anchor reframed as a text-grep against the canonical git-tracked
  template file (`fs.readFile(TEMPLATE_PATH)` + `expect(templateText).toContain(...)`).
  This is stale-clone-safe (the template is git-tracked, never stale) and still
  catches Source-class-inline-fallback vs template-default drift if either side
  changes without the other.
- Direct `templateConfig` module import was attempted but triggers `Neo.setupClass`
  namespace collision under `unitTestMode` (both config.mjs + config.template.mjs
  register the same className). Text-grep avoids the namespace registry entirely.
- Override + missing-key runtime tests still mutate `aiConfig.sourcePaths` to verify
  resolution behavior. Added defensive `if (!aiConfig.sourcePaths) aiConfig.sourcePaths = {}`
  in beforeAll so stale-clone fresh test runs don't crash on the mutations. afterAll
  restores the original state (undefined or otherwise) so clone-freshness leaks
  don't propagate to subsequent specs.

Test verification: 36 passed in 631ms post-fix.

Substrate-evolution insight: the byte-equivalence anchor design is the right
intent (detect cross-file drift) but the implementation has to be careful about
what it's actually asserting. Asserting on the live config conflates "fresh
clone" with "no drift". Asserting on the canonical template separates concerns.
